title: DTO Pattern — Student API
description:
### Your Task

Build a Student CRUD API using the DTO pattern. Another rep to build muscle memory.

### Requirements

- `Student` entity: `id` (Long), `firstName` (String), `lastName` (String), `email` (String), `grade` (String), `gpa` (Double)
- `StudentRequestDTO`: `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`, `grade`, `gpa`
- `StudentResponseDTO`: `id`, `displayName` (grade + " - " + firstName + " " + lastName), `email`, `gpa`
- `StudentController` at `/api/students`

### Endpoints

- `GET /api/students` → List of StudentResponseDTO (200)
- `GET /api/students/{id}` → StudentResponseDTO (200 or 404)
- `POST /api/students` → Accept StudentRequestDTO, return StudentResponseDTO (201)
- `PUT /api/students/{id}` → Accept StudentRequestDTO, return StudentResponseDTO (200 or 404)
- `DELETE /api/students/{id}` → 204 or 404

### Key Concepts

- Computed field: `displayName` = grade + " - " + firstName + " " + lastName
- Example: grade="10th", firstName="John", lastName="Doe" → displayName="10th - John Doe"
hints: displayName is computed during entity → response DTO mapping
The request DTO should NOT have id or displayName
Map manually: no need for MapStruct at this level
